title: Eden Emulator - Citron and Citron Neo Support
author: skalfate
body:
Hi :)

I personally really appreciate that this project exists. Thank you so much to the author and other contributors of this project.

I am hoping someday VxKex can make the latest versions of these Switch Emulators open and run. Eden  crashes and doesn't even "open" fully into the GUI, and Citron doesnt run any games, and Citron Neo crashes as well.

I don't know if it is a QT 6 compatibility issue. Although the crash event states its a "ucrtbase.dll" related issue.

Eden had once mentioned trying to support Windows 7 but I think they regressed and unintentionally went the opposite direction.

**Environment**
-
- Windows 7 SP1 x64
- Updated 2023
- SHA-2 updates installed
- Universal CRT installed
- vsc++ 2015-2022 14.42.34438.0
- Latest VxKex NEXT release

**Application**
- 
- Eden
- Citron
- Citron Neo

**Crash**
-
Faulting application name: eden.exe, version: 0.0.0.0, time stamp: 0x697ced42
Faulting module name: ucrtbase.DLL, version: 10.0.19041.789, time stamp: 0x2bd748bf
**Exception code: 0x40000015**
Fault offset: 0x000000000007287f
Faulting process id: 0x3fd4
Faulting application start time: 0x01dd09914bd7f17a

 0x40000015 means STATUS_FATAL_APP_EXIT. Your app causes an unhandled runtime exception during shutdown. Some runtime exceptions are actually handled if you don't handle them yourself, and some of these default handlers call abort(). By default, abort calls:

**Other Information**
-
 Maybe this link can help you https://github.com/huangqinjin/ucrt. It has ucrt source code at the bottom of the readme.

 This may also help https://github.com/win32ss/win32-api-reversals

title: Still not so clean. The reconing. Part 23. The sequel.

> You​

title: Support for Steam Networking Sockets/Team Fortress 2
author: TheRealAyCe
body:
It appears that Steam Networking Sockets does things that cause the Winsock API to reject it.

In Team Fortress 2, the console is filled with entries such as:

```
[SteamNetworkingSockets] WSASendTo 155.133.238.178:27060 failed, returned -1, last error=0x2726 Ein ung?ltiges Argument wurde angegeben.
```

translation: "An invalid argument was provided"

Would it be possible to catch whatever weird parameter they are using and make it work again?
This causes matchmaking to fail.

I only noticed it with TF2 so far, but I guess this affects all games using SteamNetworkingSockets?

title: teardown game support
author: TotallyARussian
body:
teardown seems to have silently dropped support after their switch to dx12 in 2023 https://steamdb.info/patchnotes/12701647/, only the 1.4 build of the game works, the pre-dx12 one.
dx12 in this case i think is probably irrelevant, the game has an openGL option, the problem is that it seems like they've also implemented microsoft's game input for windows API into the game as well.
the main teardown.exe just spits out a 0xc000005 error, while a different executable called teardown_modtest.exe asks for a gameinput.dll.
i've downloaded said gameinput.dll from one of the dll websites and it started asking for api-ms-win-downlevel-advspi32-l1-1-1.dll and after i downloaded that one it asks for api-ms-win-downlevel-advspi32-l2-1-1.dll, which i haven't been able to find a 64bit version of on the internet to try out.
interestingly enough, if i turn off vxkex for the teardown_modtest.exe, it instead asks for api-ms-win-downlevel-advspi32-l1-1-0.dll, so vxkex does seem to provide the first part of those dlls, just not the rest of them.
i've heard people on older windows 10 builds also suffering from the same gameinput.dll error in fortnite too, so that game probably also suffers from the same problem as this one.
